---
title: "Projects"
---

# Project Name: QEvent Application

The **QEvent Application** is a specialized system built with R and Shiny for event logic and response tracking. It utilizes a country detection algorithm and a SQLite backend to manage participant questions in real-time. It allows speakers to filter, classify, and display audience questions during live events.

### Live Application

You can interact with the live system below.

<iframe src="https://qevent.shinyapps.io/QEvent/" width="100%" height="800px" style="border:none;">

</iframe>

## AI Extension (In Progress)

I am developing an AI module that:

- Detects duplicate questions using text embeddings  
- Classifies questions by topic  
- Suggests answers based on historical responses  
- Prevents repetitive submissions  
- Displays questions and answers live on screen  

### Why This Matters
Large meetings often suffer from repeated questions and missed answers. This system reduces redundancy, improves clarity, and enhances the experience for both speakers and participants.

### Technologies
- **Frontend:** R + Shiny  
- **Database:** SQLite / PostgreSQL  
- **AI Engine:** Embedding models for semantic similarity  
- **Documentation:** Quarto  

---

# Flight Delay Prediction (DFW)

A predictive modeling project analyzing weather patterns, operational factors, and propagated delays to forecast flight delays at DFW Airport.

**Tech:** Python, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit‑learn, Lasso, Random Forest
